root_text.sn48.53:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.sn48.53:1.4: “atthi nu kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma sekho bhikkhu sekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘sekhosmī’ti pajāneyya, asekho bhikkhu asekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘asekhosmī’ti pajāneyyā”ti?
root_text.sn48.53:2.2: “atthi, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma sekho bhikkhu sekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘sekhosmī’ti pajāneyya, asekho bhikkhu asekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘asekhosmī’ti pajāneyya.
root_text.sn48.53:3.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma sekho bhikkhu sekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘sekhosmī’ti pajānāti?
root_text.sn48.53:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, sekho bhikkhu ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti—
root_text.sn48.53:3.3: ayampi kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma sekho bhikkhu sekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘sekhosmī’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn48.53:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, sekho bhikkhu iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.sn48.53:4.2: ‘atthi nu kho ito bahiddhā añño samaṇo vā brāhmaṇo vā yo evaṁ bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ tathaṁ dhammaṁ deseti yathā bhagavā’ti?
root_text.sn48.53:4.4: ‘natthi kho ito bahiddhā añño samaṇo vā brāhmaṇo vā yo evaṁ bhūtaṁ tacchaṁ tathaṁ dhammaṁ deseti yathā bhagavā’ti.
root_text.sn48.53:4.5: Ayampi kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma sekho bhikkhu sekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘sekhosmī’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn48.53:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, sekho bhikkhu pañcindriyāni pajānāti—
root_text.sn48.53:5.3: Yaṅgatikāni yaṁparamāni yaṁphalāni yaṁpariyosānāni. Na heva kho kāyena phusitvā viharati;
root_text.sn48.53:5.5: Ayampi kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma sekho bhikkhu sekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘sekhosmī’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn48.53:6.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma asekho bhikkhu asekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘asekhosmī’ti pajānāti?
root_text.sn48.53:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, asekho bhikkhu pañcindriyāni pajānāti—
root_text.sn48.53:6.4: Yaṅgatikāni yaṁparamāni yaṁphalāni yaṁpariyosānāni. Kāyena ca phusitvā viharati;
root_text.sn48.53:6.6: Ayampi kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma asekho bhikkhu asekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘asekhosmī’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn48.53:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, asekho bhikkhu cha indriyāni pajānāti.
root_text.sn48.53:7.3: imāni kho cha indriyāni sabbena sabbaṁ sabbathā sabbaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjhissanti, aññāni ca cha indriyāni na kuhiñci kismiñci uppajjissantī’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn48.53:7.4: Ayampi kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma asekho bhikkhu asekhabhūmiyaṁ ṭhito ‘asekhosmī’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn48.53:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.sn48.53:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn48.53:1.4: “Mendicants, is there a way that a mendicant who is a trainee, standing at the level of a trainee, can understand that they are a trainee? And that a mendicant who is an adept, standing at the level of an adept, can understand that they are an adept?”
translation_text.sn48.53:2.1: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn48.53:2.2: “There is a way that a mendicant who is a trainee, standing at the level of a trainee, can understand that they are a trainee, and that a mendicant who is an adept, standing at the level of an adept, can understand that they are an adept.
translation_text.sn48.53:3.1: And what is a way that a mendicant who is a trainee can understand that they are a trainee?
translation_text.sn48.53:3.2: It’s when a mendicant who is a trainee truly understands: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.sn48.53:3.3: This is a way that a mendicant who is a trainee can understand that they are a trainee.
translation_text.sn48.53:4.1: Furthermore, a mendicant who is a trainee reflects:
translation_text.sn48.53:4.2: ‘Is there any other ascetic or brahmin elsewhere whose teaching is as true, as real, as accurate as that of the Buddha?’
translation_text.sn48.53:4.4: ‘There is no other ascetic or brahmin elsewhere whose teaching is as true, as real, as accurate as that of the Buddha.’
translation_text.sn48.53:4.5: This too is a way that a mendicant who is a trainee can understand that they are a trainee.
translation_text.sn48.53:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant who is a trainee understands the five faculties:
translation_text.sn48.53:5.2: faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.53:5.3: And although they don’t have direct meditative experience of their destination, apex, fruit, and culmination,
translation_text.sn48.53:5.4: they do see them with penetrating wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.53:5.5: This too is a way that a mendicant who is a trainee can understand that they are a trainee.
translation_text.sn48.53:6.1: And what is the way that a mendicant who is an adept can understand that they are an adept?
translation_text.sn48.53:6.2: It’s when a mendicant who is an adept understands the five faculties:
translation_text.sn48.53:6.3: faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.53:6.4: They have direct meditative experience of their destination, apex, fruit, and culmination,
translation_text.sn48.53:6.5: and they see them with penetrating wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.53:6.6: This is a way that a mendicant who is an adept can understand that they are an adept.
translation_text.sn48.53:7.1: Furthermore, a mendicant who is an adept understands the six faculties:
translation_text.sn48.53:7.2: eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, and mind.
translation_text.sn48.53:7.3: They understand: ‘These six faculties will totally and utterly cease without anything left over. And no other six faculties will arise anywhere anyhow.’
translation_text.sn48.53:7.4: This too is a way that a mendicant who is an adept can understand that they are an adept.”
